attributes.stem: The ulnar artery is a major blood vessel in the forearm that supplies blood to the hand and forearm. It has a specific anatomical course as it travels through the forearm.
attributes.stemQuestion: Which one of the following best describes the course of the ulnar artery in the forearm?
options: Runs underneath the flexor digitorum superficialis and between flexor digitorum profundus and flexor carpi ulnaris
Runs underneath the flexor carpi ulnaris and between flexor digitorum profundus and flexor digitorum superficialis
Runs underneath the flexor digitorum profundus and between flexor digitorum superficialis and flexor carpi ulnaris
Runs underneath the flexor carpi ulnaris and between flexor digitorum superficialis and flexor digitorum profundus
Runs underneath the pronator teres and between flexor digitorum superficialis and flexor digitorum profundus
attributes.explanation:
The correct course of the ulnar artery in the forearm is that it runs underneath the pronator teres and between flexor digitorum superficialis and flexor digitorum profundus. This anatomical pathway is important for understanding the vascular supply to the forearm and hand.

The incorrect options can be explained:

  • Runs underneath the flexor carpi ulnaris and between flexor digitorum profundus and flexor digitorum superficialis - The ulnar artery does not run underneath the flexor carpi ulnaris
  • Runs underneath the flexor digitorum profundus and between flexor digitorum superficialis and flexor carpi ulnaris - The ulnar artery does not run underneath the flexor digitorum profundus
  • Runs underneath the flexor digitorum superficialis and between flexor digitorum profundus and flexor carpi ulnaris - The ulnar artery does not run underneath the flexor digitorum superficialis
  • Runs underneath the flexor carpi ulnaris and between flexor digitorum superficialis and flexor digitorum profundus - The ulnar artery does not run underneath the flexor carpi ulnaris
learningPoints: The ulnar artery in the forearm runs underneath the pronator teres muscle.
The ulnar artery then travels between the flexor digitorum superficialis and the flexor digitorum profundus muscles.
Understanding the anatomical course of the ulnar artery is important for clinical procedures involving the forearm.
references: West, S. G. (2019). Anatomy and physiology of the musculoskeletal system. Rheumatology Secrets E-Book: Rheumatology Secrets E-Book, 17
Miniato MA, Anand P, Varacallo M. Anatomy, Shoulder and Upper Limb, Shoulder. 2023 Jul 24. In: StatPearls [Internet]. Treasure Island (FL): StatPearls Publishing; 2024 Jan–. PMID: 30725618
Hutson, M., & Ward, A. (Eds.). (2015). Oxford textbook of musculoskeletal medicine. Oxford University Press

Raszewski JA, Black AC, Launico MV, et al. Anatomy, Shoulder and Upper Limb, Hand Compartments. [Updated 2023 Oct 24]. In: StatPearls [Internet]. Treasure Island (FL): StatPearls Publishing; 2024 Jan-. Available from: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sites/books/NBK532942/

McCausland C, Sawyer E, Eovaldi BJ, Varacallo M. Anatomy, Shoulder and Upper Limb, Shoulder Muscles. 2023 Aug 8. In: StatPearls [Internet]. Treasure Island (FL): StatPearls Publishing; 2024 Jan–. PMID: 30521257
Paulsen, F., & Waschke, J. (Eds.). (2023). Sobotta Atlas of Anatomy, Vol. 1, English/Latin: General Anatomy and Musculoskeletal System (Vol. 1). Elsevier Health Sciences250
